title: DenialEnvelope
description: The model-visible `knotrust` block of a synthesized tools/call denial result (P0-E5-T4, rulings R74/R75/R79). This schema validates ONLY the structural shape — `additionalProperties: false` and a closed `reasonCode` enum keep the object from ever carrying a field shaped to hold policy internals or an approval token/URL. It CANNOT forbid a token hiding inside `humanApproval.hint`'s free-text string value; that is the job of the separate, reusable frame-scan assertion (`@knotrust/test-harness`'s `assertNoLeakedSecrets`/`findLeaks`, invariant §4.3) that scans actual rendered content for token-shaped and policy-internal-shaped substrings. Schema = structure; frame-scan = content. Every envelope `buildDenialEnvelope` (packages/proxy-stdio/src/denial-envelope.ts) produces round-trips through this schema.

outcome.description: Never "allow" — an allow is transparent pass-through with no envelope of ours.

decisionId.description: ULID; the audit anchor (packages/core's DecisionResponse.decisionId, carried through verbatim).

tierClass.description: The resolved tier — safe to surface verbatim: a closed 3-value enum, never a policy id or rule name.

reasonCode.description: The R75 SAFE-subset mapping of the internal reasonCode — never the internal machine-stable code itself (e.g. never "tier_cap_violation", "envelope_deny", "no_grant_sensitive"). toSafeReasonCode() is the exhaustive, typechecked mapping function; the full internal reasonCode goes to the audit layer only.

humanApproval.description: The ONLY hint about how a human unblocks this call — a channel name, never a URL or token.

hint.description: Fixed-template prose (e.g. "Approve via the KnoTrust prompt or `knotrust approvals`"). Free text — this schema cannot forbid a token hiding inside it; see the frame-scan note above.

requestable.description: Present only for a requestable sensitive-tier deny (no covering grant). `how` names the tool/server by NAME ONLY — never an argument value, never a token/URL.

how.description: Exact CLI invocation template, e.g. "knotrust grant --tool <tool> --server <server>".

approvalId.description: Opaque approval-task handle id (e.g. "apr_<ULID>") when the decision carries one. Never an approval TOKEN (§I2.2) — an id is a lookup key with no authority of its own; a token is bearer authority and never appears here or anywhere else model-visible.

auditRef.description: Where the human/admin finds the full rationale in the audit log (today: the same value as decisionId, the audit anchor).
